title: Project Documentation & README
content:
## Overview

The project maintains a single `README.md` at the repo root as the authoritative source of documentation. Two previously existing extra README files (`README2.md`, `README3.md`) were deleted and their relevant content merged into `README.md`.

A `CLAUDE.md` file also exists at the repo root, providing project-level guidance for AI agents.

---

## README.md

### Structure

The README was rewritten top-to-bottom (commit `4b0f9fd7`) based on a direct inventory of the source files. It contains:

- **Headline description** — leads with the isometric 3D board and the expanding grid mechanic as the defining features of this snake implementation
- **Game modes section** — documents all four entry points: single player, two-player PvP, solo bot, and bot-vs-bot
- **Bot strategy table** — covers all 11 bots
- **Controls table** — includes rotation, perspective toggle, overhead view, Enter, and Esc keys
- **Quick-start instructions** — covers both development and production builds
- **Coming soon section** — trimmed to only genuinely unshipped items

### Headline / distinctive features

The expanding grid mechanic and adjustable starting grid resolution are surfaced prominently as the most distinctive behaviors of this snake implementation compared to a standard clone. This sets expectations for evaluators and contributors.

### Shipped features (in main feature list)

- Isometric 3D board
- Two-player PvP mode
- Expanding grid with adjustable starting grid size
- High score persistence
- Pause/resume
- Dark theme

"Isometric 3D board" and "Two-player PvP mode" were previously listed as "coming soon" despite being fully implemented; they were moved into the main feature list during the rewrite.

### Coming soon (roadmap)

Only unimplemented items are listed here:

- Color blind mode (requested in user feedback; deduplicated to one entry)
- First-person view (user-requested: see the game from the snake's own perspective)
- Possible rename to "snek"
- Global/online leaderboard — distinct from the local per-mode leaderboards that already exist
- 🌈 Rainbow snake surprise (teased in commit `ea18bf87`; not yet implemented)

---

## CLAUDE.md

Created in commit `7fdbb7ae`. Contains a **"Use Jolli Memory"** section instructing Claude to:

- Recall prior branch context before planning or coding
- Search across branches when design questions arise
- Use the Jolli PR skill when opening pull requests

### Why a project-level file

A project-level guidance file is loaded into context every session and applies to anyone working in the repo. A memory entry would not be reliably loaded; a hook would be too rigid for a "when relevant" guideline and would misfire on unrelated tasks.

---

## Content consolidation decisions

- `README2.md` and `README3.md` were deleted; their content was merged into the "Coming soon" section of `README.md`
- `README3.md` originally held raw user feedback (color blind mode, viewing other players' high scores, first-person view) recorded verbatim without product decisions; these were triaged into the roadmap on merge
- Redundant framing (separate "future plans" and "user feedback" headers) was dropped rather than preserved as subsections
- The overlapping color blind mode request that appeared in both deleted files was deduplicated to a single roadmap entry
- Global leaderboards were explicitly re-added to the roadmap (not dropped) because they represent a genuine user request distinct from existing local leaderboards
